question.text: In the Indian derivatives market, the options traded are:

answer.text: European options, which can be exercised only on the expiry date
American options, which can be exercised any time before expiry
Both European and American options depending on the exchange
Bermuda options, which can be exercised on specific dates before expiry

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** In the Indian markets, only European options are currently traded. A European option can be exercised only on the expiry date. An American option can be exercised at any time before or on the expiry date. Contract codes indicate the type: CE = Call European, PE = Put European. Note: Per SEBI circular (May 2025), the expiry day may be the last Tuesday or Thursday of the month.
**The Trap:** American options are not currently traded in Indian stock exchanges despite the name suggesting otherwise. Bermuda options are exotic options not available on Indian exchanges. The terminology can confuse — "European" and "American" refer to exercise style, not geography.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Derivatives basics overlap with Series VIII, XIII. The European-only nature of Indian exchange-traded options is a commonly tested fact.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** European options are simpler to understand and price. Since they can only be exercised on expiry, the decision is straightforward — exercise if ITM on the last day, otherwise let it expire. This makes them more accessible for new derivatives participants.
